title: Navigate the Quran by Juz (Para): All 30 Parts with Arabic, Translations and Audio
excerpt: The Quran's 30-Juz (Para) structure is the most practical navigation system for daily reading and Ramadan Khatm. Explore each Juz with full Arabic text, multiple translations, and audio recitations.
author: QuranBookk
content:
## The Word That Carries a World

Across South Asia — Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, and their diaspora communities worldwide — the Quran is not divided into 30 Juzs. It is divided into 30 *Paras*.

The word *Para* (پارہ) is Persian, meaning *portion* or *piece*. It entered Islamic vocabulary through the Persian-speaking Islamic world and became the standard term for the 30-part Quran division in the entire South Asian tradition. A child in Karachi learning the Quran does not complete the 30th Juz'. They complete the 30th Para. An elderly woman in Lahore beginning her Ramadan Khatm does not open Juz' 1. She opens Para 1.

Both words describe the same thing: the 30 equal portions into which the Quran was divided for systematic daily recitation. The [QuranBookk Quran Juzs](/quran/juzs/) feature covers all 30 — complete with Arabic text, multiple translations, and audio recitations, serving both traditions.

## Why the 30-Juz Structure Is the Most Practical Quran Navigation System

The Quran has 114 Surahs — but their sizes vary enormously. Surah Al-Baqarah alone covers nearly a quarter of the second Juz'. Surah Al-Ikhlas is three verses. Navigating the Quran by Surah produces unequal reading sessions.

The 30-Juz division solves this completely. Each Juz' is approximately equal in length — about 20 pages of the standard Mushaf, approximately 600–700 verses. This uniformity makes the Juz' the natural unit for:

- **Daily reading targets** — one Juz' per day is the standard Ramadan Khatm schedule
- **Group Khatm** — divide the 30 Juzs among 30 people; each reads one; the group completes the full Quran in a single sitting
- **Weekly schedules** — 4–5 Juzs per week completes the Quran in about 6–7 weeks
- **Progress tracking** — in a page-based Mushaf, each Juz' starts on a clear page

## The Ramadan Khatm: Mathematics Made Simple

The most widely practiced Ramadan worship goal is the Khatm al-Quran — completing the recitation of the full Quran during the month. The 30-Juz structure makes this mathematically elegant:

- Ramadan has 29 or 30 days
- The Quran has 30 Juzs
- One Juz' per day = full Khatm completed

Each Juz' takes approximately 45 minutes to recite at a moderate pace (measured by the recitations of renowned reciters). Divided across the day — 15 minutes after Fajr, 15 minutes after Asr or Maghrib, 15 minutes before Isha or Tahajjud — one Juz' per day becomes entirely achievable for a working Muslim.

<Callout type="tip">
For Ramadan Khatm using the Juzs feature: Note which Juz' you need to read each day (Juz' 1 on Day 1, Juz' 2 on Day 2, etc.). Listen to the audio recitation of the Juz' during your commute or break, then read the Arabic text with translation in the evening. The dual approach — listening and reading — produces both Tilawah reward and comprehension.
</Callout>

## The 30 Juzs: At a Glance

Each Juz' begins at a specific verse and ends at another. Key Juzs to know:

- **Juz' 1** — Al-Fatihah (complete) + Al-Baqarah 1–141. Covers the foundations of faith and worship.
- **Juz' 2** — Al-Baqarah 142–252. The Qibla change, fasting commandment, and Ayat al-Kursi.
- **Juz' 18** — Al-Mu'minun, An-Nur, Al-Furqan. Includes the Light Verse (Ayat an-Nur) — one of the most meditated-upon verses in the Quran.
- **Juz' 28** — Al-Mujadila through At-Tahrim. Covers the final revelations from Medina.
- **Juz' 29** — Al-Mulk through Al-Mursalat. Juz' 29 is dense with short, powerful Makkan Surahs about the afterlife, creation, and divine power.
- **Juz' 30 (Juz' Amma)** — An-Naba' through An-Nas. The most memorised Juz' in the world — the short Surahs recited daily in prayers by Muslims everywhere.

## Using Juzs Alongside Other Quran Features

The Juz' structure and the Surah structure are complementary navigation systems. The [Quran Surahs feature](/quran/surahs/) lets you explore the Quran as a collection of complete literary units — ideal for studying specific Surahs deeply. The Juzs feature lets you maintain a daily reading pace — ideal for Khatm.

For the page-by-page Mushaf reading experience — where both Surah and Juz' boundaries are visually marked on each page — the [Digital Quran](/digital-quran) is the most faithful reading environment. For listening to any Juz' recited in full, the [Quran Audio collection](/quran/audio/) provides complete recitations by world-renowned reciters.

## 30 Juzs. All 30 Paras. Complete Arabic, Translations, and Audio. Free.

The [QuranBookk Quran Juzs feature](/quran/juzs/) serves every Muslim — whether you call them Juzs or Paras, whether you are beginning a Ramadan Khatm or maintaining a year-round reading schedule. Free, no account, any browser.
